summary: Rubikon Center operators conducted FPV air defense operations against Ukrainian Armed Forces fixed-wing UAVs in the Donbas and Krasny Liman directions on March 6 and 7, 2026, destroying 107 and 88 targets respectively, with over 1,500 fixed-wing UAVs intercepted to date, including 343 Leleka-100, 132 Fury, and 98 other models. ZALA Lancet loitering munitions operators successfully struck group targets despite electronic warfare interference, maximizing strike efficiency. Lasar’s Group SOF repelled mechanized assaults by the enemy in the Novopavlivka and Dobropillia directions in late January, destroying enemy tanks. Iran’s new subsonic loitering air defense missile, the Missile 358, equipped with thermal imaging homing heads, demonstrated high effectiveness against Israeli Hermes-900 UAVs. Ukraine launched Mission Control, a centralized drone management system, to enhance battlefield coordination and operational oversight.
